skill.name: quick-quality-check
skill.description: Runs fast code quality checks by coordinating theater detection, style review, security scanning, tests, and token analysis.
skill.summary: Run parallel code quality checks and summarize issues by severity.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: The static external command findings are true positives because the skill instructs agents to run npx, parallel, cat, and jq commands against a user-provided path. This appears to be legitimate quality-check behavior, but it needs a marketplace warning because shell execution and quoted path interpolation can create command-injection risk if implemented unsafely. The weak cryptography and system reconnaissance alerts are false positives from documentation text, with no evidence of cryptographic code, host enumeration, data exfiltration, or prompt injection.

medium_findings.title: External Command Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run shell commands including npx claude-flow, GNU parallel, cat, and jq. This is not malicious by itself, but it can execute repository-controlled tooling and may run network-installed packages if dependencies are not already present.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The bash blocks clearly direct command execution for quality checks. The commands match the skill purpose, so the risk is operational rather than evidence of malware.

medium_findings.title: Potential Path Injection in Shell Examples
medium_findings.description: The parallel command examples place a user-provided path variable inside shell command strings. If implemented literally with untrusted input, a crafted path could break quoting and alter the executed command.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The injection concern depends on how an agent or wrapper expands the path. The pattern is visible, but the file is instructional markdown rather than executable source code.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptography at lines that contain the skill description, the Cascades heading, and the Failure Modes heading. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, cipher selection, password hashing, or any cryptographic implementation in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are prose headings or metadata, not cryptographic code. The surrounding context contains no cryptographic operations.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alert Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged line 16, which states the role of a quality assurance specialist. No evidence found of host enumeration, environment probing, credential discovery, or system inventory commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is descriptive role text. The only operational commands are quality-check commands shown later in the document.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Commands With User-Provided Path
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill shows shell commands that consume a path parameter and write report files. Marketplace users should run it only in trusted repositories and validate paths before command execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The path parameter is directly incorporated into command strings in the documented workflow. The risk is credible when translated into an automated wrapper.

content.value_statement: Code teams need quick feedback before deeper reviews or releases. This skill coordinates parallel quality checks and returns a severity-ranked report.
actual_capabilities: Coordinates theater detection, style audit, security scan, test coverage, and token usage checks.
Uses parallel execution patterns to reduce quality-check turnaround time.
Aggregates separate check results into one severity-ranked quality report.
Supports checks for a file path or directory path supplied by the user.
Escalates poor scores or security issues to deeper review workflows.
limitations: Requires compatible claude-flow commands or equivalent local automation to be available.
The documented workflow executes shell commands and should run only in trusted repositories.
Quick mode may miss issues that require deeper static analysis or full test suites.
Report quality depends on the configured linters, scanners, and test tools.

use_cases.title: Pre-Review Quality Sweep
use_cases.description: Run a fast quality check before asking for a human or AI code review.

use_cases.title: Release Readiness Gate
use_cases.description: Check style, test, and security signals before promoting a build candidate.

use_cases.title: Repository Health Triage
use_cases.description: Find placeholders, weak tests, and security warnings across a repository snapshot.

prompt_templates.title: Check Current Project
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner check for the active repository.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use quick-quality-check on the current project. Summarize critical, high, medium, and low issues.

prompt_templates.title: Check One Directory
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused review for a changed source area.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run quick-quality-check on the src directory in quick mode. Highlight blockers first and include test signals.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Before Release
prompt_templates.prompt: Use quick-quality-check for release readiness. Prioritize security findings, failing tests, and placeholder code.

prompt_templates.title: Triage With Escalation
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow that routes risky results to deeper review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run quick-quality-check with detailed output. If the score is poor or security issues appear, recommend the next deep audit step.

output_examples.input: Check the current repository before opening a pull request.
output: One high issue needs review before merge.
Style and token checks passed with minor warnings.

output_examples.input: Run a quick check on the src directory for release readiness.
output: Security scan found no critical issues.
Tests passed, but coverage is below the configured target.
Two placeholder comments should be resolved before release.

output_examples.input: Audit a single API file for fast feedback.
output: The file passed linting and formatting checks.
One medium security warning needs validation.

best_practices: Run the skill from a clean working tree so generated reports are easy to review.
Use trusted repositories and validate paths before allowing shell command execution.
Follow quick checks with deeper review when security or release blockers appear.
anti_patterns: Do not use quick mode as the only security review for sensitive systems.
Do not run the command examples against untrusted paths or unreviewed repositories.
Do not treat a high score as proof that production readiness is complete.

faq.question: What does this skill check?
faq.answer: It checks code theater signals, style, security warnings, basic tests, coverage, and token usage signals.

faq.question: Does it execute commands?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented workflow uses shell commands, so users should run it only in trusted project environments.

faq.question: Is it safe for untrusted repositories?
faq.answer: No. Review the repository and command configuration before running external commands on untrusted code.

faq.question: Can it replace a full security audit?
faq.answer: No. It provides fast triage and should escalate serious findings to deeper security review.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What should I do with low scores?
faq.answer: Review the severity-ranked findings, fix blockers first, and run a deeper audit for persistent quality failures.
